af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. SCIM 2.0 (System for Cross-domain Identity Management) is an IETF standard REST API for automated user provisioning across identity providers (Okta, Azure AD, Google, etc.). Bearer token auth via OAuth2 — token obtained from identity provider. SCIM is implemented by enterprise IdPs; actual rate limits depend on the hosting provider. Strong schema validation (required vs. optional attributes). PATCH operations use complex JSON Patch format. Filter syntax for user/group queries follows SCIM spec. Bulk operations endpoint supports batch provisioning.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory across all SCIM 2.0 implementations. OAuth2 Bearer token authentication — strong standard. SCIM scopes map to user/group read and write permissions. Credential handling via Bearer tokens in Authorization header. Enterprise compliance (SOC2, HIPAA, GDPR) typically enforced by IdP hosting the SCIM endpoint. Token via environment variable is standard practice. SCIM is explicitly designed for enterprise security requirements — identity data handled with high security standards.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SCIM 2.0 (RFC 7643/7644) is a stable IETF standard — no breaking changes to the specification. SLA depends on the IdP provider (e.g., Okta has enterprise SLA). Retry guidance varies by provider. Bulk operations provide atomicity options. Pagination uses cursor-based 'startIndex' parameter per spec. Provisioning failures return structured error objects per SCIM spec. Extremely mature standard with decade of production use across enterprise identity.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 58). Python web scraping library with anti-bot bypass capabilities (Playwright/requests backends, stealth mode, fingerprint rotation). No authentication needed for the library itself — auth_complexity_score=85 (API key-style config). IMPORTANT: Designed to circumvent anti-bot protections — ToS compliance is caller's responsibility. Target site rate limits apply but not documented. CSS/XPath selectors plus smart element detection. Dynamic content requires Playwright backend. Error handling is basic with minimal structured errors.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for target sites (TLS depends on target). No authentication layer on the tool itself — runs as local library. Fingerprint rotation and stealth mode are designed to evade security systems — dual-use security concern. Python dependencies include Playwright and browser binaries — significant attack surface requiring audit. Proxy credentials can be passed in config — verify these aren't logged. Anti-detection features may violate target site ToS. No formal security audit. Community project.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local Python library — no SLA. Anti-bot bypass techniques can break without notice when targets update their defenses. Playwright backend reliability depends on browser version compatibility. No retry guidance documented. Early project with growing adoption but limited production track record at scale. Target site changes are the primary reliability risk. Browser automation adds complexity for headless environments.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Twilio Segment Customer Data Platform — event tracking, identity resolution, and data routing. Write Keys for event ingestion (source-specific), Personal Access Tokens for management API (workspace-scoped). Two separate API surfaces: Tracking API (event ingestion) and Public API (workspace management, OpenAPI spec available). Batch event sends for efficiency. Identify, Track, Page, Screen, Group calls have different schemas. Destination filtering and transformation logic can produce unexpected behavior. Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Write Keys (source-specific, limited blast radius) and Personal Access Tokens (workspace-scoped management). SOC2 Type II, GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA compliant — Twilio enterprise security posture. Write Key vs management token separation follows principle of least privilege. Tokens via environment variables. Webhook delivery signatures for integration security. Strong compliance framework for customer data handling.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Twilio Segment status page and enterprise SLA. Stable Tracking API v1 and Public API v1beta. Retry guidance documented. Event ingestion is async — events may batch before delivery to destinations. Batch event sends provide efficiency and atomicity options. Cursor-based pagination on management API. Destination failures don't propagate back to source — monitor destination health separately. Very reliable as core customer data infrastructure.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Semgrep Cloud Platform REST API for managing SAST scans, rules, findings, and security policies. Bearer token authentication — simple for agents. Semgrep OSS (CLI) is free; API access requires Semgrep Team or Enterprise plan. Findings have states (open, ignored, fixed) requiring correct state management. Custom rule deployment via API is powerful but requires valid rule YAML. Deployment IDs required for scan management. Rate limits not prominently documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Bearer token with org/project scoped access. SOC2 Type II and GDPR compliant. Security-focused company with strong internal security practices. Token via Authorization header/env vars. API handles security findings — appropriate sensitivity. Semgrep's own supply chain is audited given security product nature. No fine-grained per-resource token scoping beyond org level.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2. Stable REST API. Scans are async — agents must poll for completion. Finding state transitions are deterministic. Retry guidance not formally documented. Cursor-based pagination on findings. CLI-based scan results may differ from cloud scan results — use cloud API for consistent results. Generally reliable as security tooling platform.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Twilio SendGrid transactional and marketing email API. API key with granular permission scopes — excellent agent-friendly design. SDKs in Python, Node, Ruby, PHP, Go, C#, Java. Domain/IP warming required for new senders — agents must account for warmup period before high-volume sends. Marketing vs transactional are separate API surfaces. Dynamic templates use Handlebars syntax. Unsubscribe group management required for compliance. Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. API keys with fine-grained per-permission scopes (send mail, manage templates, view analytics, etc.) — excellent least-privilege design. SOC2 Type II, GDPR, ISO27001, PCI-DSS compliant — Twilio enterprise security. Unsubscribe compliance built into API. API key via Authorization header/env vars. Domain authentication (DKIM/SPF) required for production. Webhook event verification available. Industry-leading email security posture.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Twilio SendGrid status page and enterprise SLA. Stable v3 API with years of backward compatibility. Retry guidance documented. IP reputation and domain warmup are critical reliability factors for new senders. Email delivery is inherently async — webhooks for delivery status. Batch send API for large volume. Activity feed for debugging deliverability issues. Very mature and reliable as core email infrastructure.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 80). Sentry error monitoring REST API with excellent documentation. Two auth mechanisms: User Auth Tokens (personal, all orgs) and Internal Integrations (org-scoped, preferred for agents). DSN is separate — for event ingestion only, not management. Issue/event queries support powerful filter syntax. Releases and deploy tracking integrate with CI/CD. Cron monitoring and performance metrics APIs available. Rate limits documented but conservative. Pagination uses cursor-based approach.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Internal Integration tokens are org-scoped with fine-grained permission scopes (read issues, write issues, manage alerts, etc.) — excellent least-privilege design for agents. SOC2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA, ISO27001 compliant. Secrets (DSN, auth tokens) via environment variables. Error data may contain PII — data scrubbing rules available. Strong enterprise security posture for a security-adjacent product.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Sentry status page and enterprise SLA. Stable API with versioned endpoints. Retry guidance documented. Cursor-based pagination. Issue ingest is highly available. Event data retention limits apply (varies by plan). Alert rule triggers are async. Integration token permissions take effect immediately. Very reliable platform — production monitoring infrastructure for millions of projects.

af_score_components.notes: Official Sentry MCP server (quality 80) built and maintained by Sentry. Provides natural language access to Sentry error monitoring — query issues, analyze errors, triage problems. Auth via Sentry Internal Integration or User Auth Token. Covers core agent workflows: find recent errors, get issue details, assign issues, create comments. Remote MCP server available at mcp.sentry.dev — no local setup required for hosted Sentry. Self-hosted Sentry users need local MCP setup.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Official Sentry-maintained server — security practices match Sentry's enterprise posture. Internal Integration tokens with org-scoped permissions preferred for agents. SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA compliance inherited from Sentry platform. Auth token via env var. Remote MCP endpoint (mcp.sentry.dev) is Sentry-operated infrastructure. Error data accessed through MCP may contain PII — same data scrubbing rules apply as REST API.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Official Sentry maintenance provides strong reliability guarantee. Remote MCP endpoint reliability tied to Sentry's infrastructure SLA. Sentry status page covers MCP endpoint. Stable tool interface following Sentry API stability. Breaking changes would be coordinated with Sentry API versioning. Generally excellent reliability given official ownership and enterprise user base.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 75). LSP-based coding agent toolkit — provides semantic code operations (find references, go-to-definition, rename symbols, diagnostics) via Language Server Protocol. auth_complexity_score=100 (no auth needed — local tool). Supports multiple languages via their LSP implementations (Python/pylsp, TypeScript/typescript-language-server, etc.). Language server must be installed and configured separately for each language. Context-aware code navigation enables precise edits vs. text-search approach. No rate limits (local tool). Error recovery depends on LSP server stability.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local tool — no external network calls for core operations. No credentials needed for code analysis. Security relies on local OS access controls. Language server processes run locally — standard local security boundary. Python dependencies and language server binaries should be audited. No API surface to secure externally. Code it analyzes stays local — no telemetry by default.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local tool — no SLA. Reliability depends on: (1) the specific LSP server installed for each language, (2) LSP server startup time, (3) project indexing completion. LSP servers have variable stability (pylsp is mature; others vary). Error recovery from LSP crashes requires restart. Early project with growing adoption. Excellent for precise semantic code operations when LSP is stable. Tier 2 language LSP quality varies significantly.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Salesforce Marketing Cloud REST and SOAP APIs — complex enterprise marketing automation platform. auth_complexity_score=28 (extremely complex) — OAuth2 with client credentials but tenant-specific auth endpoints (each SFMC tenant has unique subdomain), separate REST/SOAP endpoints, integration user required, Marketing Cloud Connect for Sales Cloud integration adds more complexity. Both REST (v1/v2) and SOAP (legacy) APIs must be understood. Data Extension operations use different endpoint patterns than messaging. AMPscript required for dynamic content. Stack-based architecture means production vs sandbox URLs differ per tenant.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. OAuth2 with client credentials flow — strong enterprise auth. Salesforce ecosystem security standards (SOC1, SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS). Integration user permissions provide granular access control. Client ID/secret via environment variables. Tenant-specific subdomain provides some isolation. Enterprise-grade security inherited from Salesforce/Trailhead trust model.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Salesforce/Trailhead status page and enterprise SLA. REST v2 is current but SOAP API still required for some features — dual-protocol complexity. Tenant-specific URL configuration means misconfiguration causes auth failures. Email sends are async with status callbacks. Rate limits (concurrency, daily send volume) enforced at tenant level. Retry complexity due to tenant-specific endpoints. SOAP API deprecation timeline unclear. Generally reliable for enterprise marketing automation once properly configured.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Multi-carrier shipping API supporting USPS, FedEx, UPS, DHL and 85+ carriers. Simple API key authentication. Test mode with test tokens for development. Rate shopping (get rates from multiple carriers simultaneously) is a key feature. Address validation before label creation prevents failed pickups. Labels expire — agents must download promptly after creation. Shipment vs Transaction objects: shipment=rate comparison, transaction=purchased label. Webhook support for tracking updates. OpenAPI spec available. SDKs in Python, Node, PHP, Ruby.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Simple API key (Shippo token) in Authorization header — no fine-grained scope control. SOC2 certified. Test vs live API keys provide environment isolation. API key via environment variables. PII data (shipping addresses) handled with SOC2 compliance. Carrier credential management (for custom carrier accounts) requires careful secrets handling. No RBAC beyond test/live key distinction.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2. Stable REST API with OpenAPI spec. Carrier availability varies — rate shopping gracefully excludes unavailable carriers. Label voiding has time limits per carrier. Tracking webhooks require endpoint registration. Retry guidance documented. Address validation failure is synchronous — immediate feedback. Non-idempotent label creation (must void and recreate). Offset-based pagination. Very reliable for core shipping workflow.
